response.justification: Bogdan is a versatile polyglot engineer with genuine developer-tooling credentials — he worked on the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments and has authored test runners and assertion libraries — but the role's core requirements are largely unmet. His career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms; Java appears only peripherally, and there is no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, or deep JVM expertise, which are hard requirements for building enterprise Java/Kotlin IDE tooling.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) and clear passion for language tooling and testing infrastructure
Experience with complex, high-scale systems (low-latency microservices handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing) and active knowledge-sharing through workshops and talks
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience, both core to the role
Java exposure is minimal and dated (side projects at Altom ~2016-2018), with no deep JVM work evident
Recent experience is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js) rather than enterprise JVM frameworks, build tools, or application servers
